title: VxRail vSAN Cluster Free Capacity and Failures-To-Tolerate Rebuild Headroom
exclusions: vSAN Express Storage Architecture (ESA) clusters use different slack and efficiency accounting than the classic OSA-style exports described here; a separate UC and poller are needed for ESA so customers do not mix algorithms. Non-VxRail vSAN ReadyNode and generic vSAN on partner hardware should use UC-19.2.2 for the vendor-agnostic pool and UC-19.2.27 for the generic slack and rebuild view without a VxRail Manager join. VxRail dynamic node clusters that place primary data on external VMware Cloud Foundation or third-party storage are out of scope for this on-cluster vSAN FTT check. Stretched, metro, or witness topologies with an active site failure or a broken witness are excluded because evacuation safety depends on fault domains and witness health, not this single-cluster linear estimate. Non-vSphere HCI stacks are a different subcategory. Do not treat this Splunk boolean as a substitute for the vSphere client maintenance pre-check UI.
evidence: Saved search or alert uc_19_2_37_vxrail_vsan_ftt_evac on index=vmware or index=hci with vxrail index union as deployed, 13+ month online retention, version-controlled vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner.csv, and a retained alert result row with matching vCenter Capacity screenshot in the ITSM or evidence pack for sampled audits.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab, ingest vmware:vsan:capacity with low free_capacity_gb, largest_host_contribution_gb near that free number, ftt_src=1, and confirm safe_to_evacuate_one_host reads no and severity0 is at least medium; add vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner ftt_required=2 to raise evacuation_storm_estimate_gb and worsen the tier, proving FTT governs the gate.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Set change_freeze_until to a future ISO timestamp in the lookup for a cluster, reload transforms, and confirm severity_bucket=info for rows that would otherwise be medium, while the table still shows safe_to_evacuate_one_host for audit clarity.

dataSources: PRIMARY: Dell EMC OpenManage Splunk Add-on when deployed for VxRail, else custom HEC from a VxRail Manager poller: GET https://{vxm-fqdn}/rest/vxm/v1/cluster and GET https://{vxm-fqdn}/rest/vxm/v1/hosts, sourcetype=vxrail:vxm:cluster and vxrail:vxm:host, index=vxrail. SECONDARY (required for real vSAN bytes and FTT): PowerCLI scheduled job (Install-Module VMware.PowerCLI) exporting Get-VsanSpaceUsage with FreeSpaceGB, TotalCapacityGB, EfficiencyDataLogicalGB, EfficiencyDataPhysicalGB, DedupAndCompressionRatio; Get-Cluster | Get-VsanClusterConfiguration for VsanEnabled, StretchedClusterEnabled, FaultDomainsEnabled; Get-VMHost | Get-VsanRuntimeInfo for per-host physical contribution; vSAN/SPBM for host_failures_to_tolerate, sourcetype=vmware:vsan:capacity, vmware:vsan:cluster_config, vmware:vsan:host_runtime, vmware:vsan:storage_policy on index=vmware or index=hci. TERTIARY: Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder (Splunkbase 5380) for vmware:perf latency during resyncs and vmware:vc:event for vSAN resync and maintenance events, drill-down only.
app: Custom HEC poller for VxRail Manager REST API; PowerCLI scheduled export of vSAN capacity to HEC; (optional) Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.37 VxRail vSAN FTT-aware one-host maintenance headroom. Tunables: index union vmware hci vxrail; inputlookup vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner; owner: HCI runbook. Change-freeze: demote all routed tiers to info. Siblings: UC-19.2.27 generic slack only, UC-19.2.26 LCM preflight gate, UC-19.2.2 generic pools.")`
| search (index=vmware OR index=hci OR index=vxrail) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:capacity" earliest=-6h@h latest=@h
| eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, vxrail_cluster_id, vsphere_cluster, vsphereCluster, ""))
| eval vcenter=toString(coalesce(vcenter, vCenter, vcenter_fqdn, vCenterFqdn, ""))
| eval free_capacity_gb=tonumber(toString(coalesce(freeSpaceGB, free_space_gb, freeCapacityGB, free_capacity_gb, "0")), 10)
| eval total_capacity_gb=tonumber(toString(coalesce(TotalCapacityGB, total_capacity_gb, totalCapacityGB, "0")), 10)
| eval slack_capacity_gb=tonumber(toString(coalesce(slackSpaceGB, slack_space_gb, slackCapacityGB, slack_capacity_gb, "0")), 10)
| eval ftt_src=tonumber(toString(coalesce(host_failures_to_tolerate, hostFailuresToTolerate, policyFtt, "0")), 10)
| eval host_count=tonumber(toString(coalesce(host_count, hostCount, "0")), 10)
| where (isnotnull(total_capacity_gb)) AND (total_capacity_gb>0)
| sort 0 + cluster_id, +_time
| streamstats current=t first(free_capacity_gb) AS fcb_start first(_time) AS t0 by cluster_id
| eval free_pct=round(100.0 * free_capacity_gb / total_capacity_gb, 2)
| eval slack_pct=if((isnotnull(slack_capacity_gb)) AND (slack_capacity_gb>0) AND (total_capacity_gb>0), round(100.0 * slack_capacity_gb / total_capacity_gb, 2), null())
| eval elapsed=if((isnotnull(t0)) AND (_time>t0), _time - t0, 0)
| eval free_capacity_trend_per_day=if((elapsed>0) AND (isnotnull(fcb_start)), (free_capacity_gb - fcb_start) * 86400.0 / elapsed, 0)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:host_runtime" earliest=-6h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval g=tonumber(toString(coalesce(physicalUsedCapacityGB, per_host_contribution_gb, usedCapacityOnHost, capacityUsedHostGB, "0")), 10)
      | where (g>0)
      | stats max(g) AS largest_host_contribution_gb BY cluster_id ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:storage_policy" earliest=-6h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval ftt_p=tonumber(toString(coalesce(host_failures_to_tolerate, hostFailuresToTolerate, "0")), 10)
      | where (isnotnull(ftt_p)) AND (ftt_p>=0)
      | stats latest(ftt_p) AS ftt_policy BY cluster_id ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [ search (index=vxrail) (sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:cluster" OR sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:host")
      earliest=-6h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval vfm=toString(coalesce(vxm_fqdn, vxmHost, vxm, manager_fqdn, ""))
      | stats max(vfm) AS vxm_fqdn_ingest BY cluster_id ]
| stats
    max(_time) AS _time
    latest(free_capacity_gb) AS free_capacity_gb
    latest(total_capacity_gb) AS total_capacity_gb
    latest(slack_capacity_gb) AS slack_capacity_gb
    latest(free_pct) AS free_pct
    latest(slack_pct) AS slack_pct
    latest(free_capacity_trend_per_day) AS free_capacity_trend_per_day
    latest(ftt_src) AS ftt_src
    latest(largest_host_contribution_gb) AS largest_host_contribution_gb
    latest(ftt_policy) AS ftt_policy
    latest(vxm_fqdn_ingest) AS vxm_fqdn_ingest
    latest(host_count) AS host_count
    latest(vcenter) AS vcenter
  BY cluster_id
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id [ | inputlookup vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner | fields cluster_id, vxm_fqdn, owner_team, target_min_free_pct, target_min_slack_pct, ftt_required, change_freeze_until ]
| eval vxm_fqdn=if((isnotnull(vxm_fqdn)) AND (len(trim(toString(vxm_fqdn)))>0), toString(vxm_fqdn), toString(vxm_fqdn_ingest))
| eval ftt_use=tonumber(toString(coalesce(ftt_required, ftt_policy, ftt_src, "1")), 10)
| eval evacuation_storm_estimate_gb=if((isnotnull(largest_host_contribution_gb)) AND (largest_host_contribution_gb>0) AND (isnotnull(ftt_use)) AND (ftt_use>=0), largest_host_contribution_gb * (ftt_use + 1), null())
| eval safe_to_evacuate_one_host=if((isnotnull(free_capacity_gb)) AND (isnotnull(evacuation_storm_estimate_gb)) AND (free_capacity_gb>evacuation_storm_estimate_gb), "yes", "no")
| eval tminf=tonumber(toString(coalesce(target_min_free_pct, "20")), 10)
| eval tmins=tonumber(toString(coalesce(target_min_slack_pct, "10")), 10)
| eval cft1=strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(trim(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(cft1, cft2, cft3, cft4, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval severity0=case(
    (safe_to_evacuate_one_host="no") AND ((isnotnull(free_pct)) AND (free_pct<tminf)) AND ((isnotnull(slack_pct)) AND (slack_pct<tmins)), "critical",
    (safe_to_evacuate_one_host="no") AND (((isnotnull(free_pct)) AND (free_pct<tminf)) OR ((isnotnull(slack_pct)) AND (slack_pct<tmins))), "high",
    (safe_to_evacuate_one_host="no") OR ((isnotnull(free_pct)) AND (free_pct<(tminf+5))) OR ((isnotnull(slack_pct)) AND (slack_pct<(tmins+3))), "medium",
    (free_capacity_trend_per_day<0) OR ((isnotnull(free_pct)) AND (free_pct<(tminf+10))), "low",
    1=1, "ok"
  )
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1), "info", severity0)
| sort 0 + severity0, -free_pct, cluster_id
| table _time, cluster_id, vcenter, vxm_fqdn, host_count, ftt_use, free_pct, slack_pct, free_capacity_gb, total_capacity_gb, largest_host_contribution_gb, evacuation_storm_estimate_gb, safe_to_evacuate_one_host, free_capacity_trend_per_day, severity0, severity_bucket, freeze_active, owner_team
description: Ingests VxRail Manager v1 cluster and host inventory alongside PowerCLI-derived vmware:vsan:capacity, vmware:vsan:host_runtime, and optional vmware:vsan:storage_policy lines, unifies FTT and largest per-host vSAN data footprint, joins governance from vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner, then computes free_pct, slack_pct, evacuation_storm_estimate_gb, and a boolean-style safe_to_evacuate_one_host (yes or no) before any rolling host LCM, DIMM, or NIC work under typical Ensure Accessibility plans—distinct from UC-19.2.13 cluster health, UC-19.2.26 bundle train compliance, UC-19.2.2 generic pool ETAs, and UC-19.2.27 vendor-neutral usable slack and rebuild bytes without a VxRail-specific FTT gate.
value: Platform teams and change approvers get an indexed, ticket-ready proof that a one-host evacuation for planned maintenance on Dell VxRail-shaped vSAN will not exhaust free space and slack during the re-protection and component movement that FTT and RAID policies imply, so maintenance windows, capacity purchases, and LCM from UC-19.2.26 are sequenced on evidence instead of a single raw free-percent gauge that ignores how much of the cluster the largest node represents.
implementation: Deploy HEC to index=vxrail for vxrail:vxm:cluster and vxrail:vxm:host, index=vmware (or hci) for the PowerCLI vmware:vsan:* family on a 30m schedule, add lookups/vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner.csv, save the `spl` as uc_19_2_37_vxrail_vsan_ftt_evac, schedule every 6h with earliest=-6h@h, and wire alert actions to the owner_team column; add optional vSphere add-on 5380 for drill-only perf and event panels.
visualization: Row 1: single value: clusters with safe_to_evacuate_one_host = no. Row 2: treemap by cluster_id colored by free_pct. Row 3: timechart of free_pct with a band for target_min_free_pct. Row 4: top-N at-risk table from the `table` clause. Drills: vmware:perf and vmware:vc:event for resync and latency, paired with this UC, not in the same severity pipeline.

references.title: Dell VxRail documentation and API (support manuals)

knownFalsePositives: A mid-fleet VxRail expansion with new disk groups can lift total_capacity_gb while logical free still looks tight until rebalancing across disk groups; safe_to_evacuate may read no for days until the cluster finishes redistribution. A rolling LCM or firmware storm can spike physical used% while resync is active, even when steady-state is fine. change_freeze windows for quarter-end that suppress paging can leave rows at severity_bucket=info that still warrant human review on paper. A hint that the cluster is on vSAN Express Storage Architecture (ESA) may invalidate older slack-pct heuristics from OSA pollers, so the numeric bands look worse than the actual admission path until you align the exporter. Snapshot-heavy backup chains can temporarily change dedupe effectiveness and the observed DedupAndCompressionRatio without a hardware failure.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites

This use case is the Dell VxRail–specific FTT-aware evacuation-safety check. It is intentionally different from UC-19.2.27, which is vendor-neutral vSAN slack and usable reserve math, and from UC-19.2.13, which is VxRail Manager cluster and host health without vSAN free-space and FTT synthesis. It also differs from UC-19.2.26, which governs LCM bundle compliance; the tie-in is procedural: a planned LCM kick should refuse to start when the boolean safe_to_evacuate_one_host from this use case is no, and that gate should be automated or enforced in the change ticket, not as a hand wave.

Index and sourcetype contract: you must allow searching index=vxrail and index=vmware (or a unified index=hci that holds both) with the following sourcetype names you document in your props.conf, because no Splunkbase app ships a complete end-to-end contract for VxRail Manager v1 and PowerCLI in one product bundle. Primary VxRail Manager REST, delivered through custom HEC: vxrail:vxm:cluster as JSON from GET https://{vxm-fqdn}/rest/vxm/v1/cluster, and vxrail:vxm:host as JSON from GET https://{vxm-fqdn}/rest/vxm/v1/hosts, including cluster_id, vxm identity when returned, and host inventory. Secondary vSAN truth from the management plane, delivered through PowerCLI or a vCenter poller, using vmware:vsan:capacity (Get-VsanSpaceUsage outputs FreeSpaceGB, TotalCapacityGB, and slack when exposed), vmware:vsan:cluster_config (Get-VsanClusterConfiguration for VsanEnabled, StretchedClusterEnabled, FaultDomainsEnabled), vmware:vsan:host_runtime (Get-VsanRuntimeInfo per host for physical contribution), and vmware:vsan:storage_policy (SPBM default and dominant policy host_failures_to_tolerate). Tertiary drill-down only: install Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder, Splunkbase 5380, to collect vmware:perf and vmware:vc:event for resync and datastore latency; these do not form part of the core severity in the `spl` field, but you place them on an operational dashboard next to the alert.

Service accounts: a VxRail Manager read-only HCIA or equivalent service account is sufficient for the two GET paths above, with no write scope. A vCenter service account for PowerCLI should have vSAN read and storage view permissions, not interactive administrator, and a dedicated Windows or Linux service principal for the scheduled export. Splunk HEC must use a token in a vault, rotated quarterly, with acknowledgement enabled on the indexer path you target.

The lookup vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner.csv (Search Head) must exist with a transforms.conf stanza [vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner] and filename=vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner.csv, min_matches=0, case-insensitive for cluster_id. Column list on the header row: cluster_id, vxm_fqdn, owner_team, target_min_free_pct, target_min_slack_pct, ftt_required, change_freeze_until. The SPL joins on cluster_id. vxm_fqdn is a governance copy of the VxRail Manager FQDN for ticket routing. owner_team is a mail-enabled group or a queue name. target_min_free_pct and target_min_slack_pct are governance lower bounds; the case ladder compares both free and slack to these after computing safe_to_evacuate_one_host. ftt_required is the FTT to assume if policy feeds disagree. change_freeze_until is ISO-8601 or a date, parsed with multiple strptime in SPL so operators can set a day-only freeze.

Optional Dell EMC OpenManage Splunk Add-on: when your org already has it, map its normalized fields to the same cluster_id you use in this search. If the add-on and custom HEC disagree, the custom HEC that you own in Git wins for this use case, because the catalogue documents field names the SPL expects, not the vendor’s internal transformation layer.

NTP, TLS, and proxy: the automation host, VxRail Manager VM, vCenter, and Splunk indexers should agree in time. TLS inspection appliances must list vCenter and VxRail in passthrough for PowerCLI, or the export script will fail on MITM. Corporate proxies should allow outbound 443 from the automation host to vCenter, VxRail, and the Splunk HEC URL.

Pillar mapping: monitoringType is Capacity and Reliability because a miss here is a dual failure mode—exhausting growth runway and failing the cluster’s own fault-tolerance story during maintenance, not a security domain problem.



Step 1 — Configure data collection

Inputs.conf (Heavy Forwarder) for HEC: create a single global or per-token stanza, example token name vxf_hec in local/inputs.conf, disabled=0, indexes=vxrail, useDeploymentServer-friendly paths. A splunkd restart is required; prefer a dedicated HEC for VxRail rather than a shared dev token, so token revocation does not take unrelated feeds offline.

A Python 3.10+ poller using requests, scheduled every five minutes on cron, should POST to https://<splunk-hec>:8088/services/collector/event with JSON wrapper {"event": flatdict, "sourcetype": "vxrail:vxm:cluster"}. Flatten the cluster JSON. For hosts, a second call or batch with sourcetype vxrail:vxm:host. Handle HTTP 401 by renewing session, 429 with exponential backoff, and 5xx with retry. Never embed passwords in a Splunk .conf; use OS-level secret stores or vault sidecars. Log poller health to a local file and optionally to HTTP Event Collector in a `vxrail:poller:health` sourcetype for Meta monitoring.

Props.conf stanzas for the four key sourcetypes you must have online before the alert: [vxrail:vxm:cluster], [vxrail:vxm:host], [vmware:vsan:capacity], [vmware:vsan:host_runtime] with SHOULD_LINEMERGE=false, TRUNCATE=0 or 2000000, INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS=json, and LINE_BREAKER=([\r\n]+). Add CHARSET=UTF-8. If a field is named `time` in the JSON, use TIME_PREFIX in props or have the HEC set _time in epoch at post time.

transforms.conf for the lookup, as in Prerequisites, plus a default.csv row you document as sample-only for Search preview.

Windows Task Scheduler: create a task every thirty minutes, highest reliability when AC power, run as the PowerCLI user, with action powershell.exe -File C:\VxrailVsan\Export-VsanToHec.ps1. The script should Connect-VIServer, enumerate each VxRail-backed cluster you govern, run Get-VsanSpaceUsage, Get-VsanClusterConfiguration, per-host Get-VsanRuntimeInfo, and optionally list SPBM policies, then build one vmware:vsan:capacity line per vSAN cluster and optional vmware:vsan:host_runtime lines, vmware:vsan:storage_policy, vmware:vsan:cluster_config. HEC with Invoke-RestMethod, TLS 1.2. Throttle the script so it does not exceed vCenter connection limits. Store last successful poll timestamp in a small state file; alert if the file is older than two intervals.

A sample block for the capacity line, illustrative only, not a warranty of field names: include cluster_id, vcenter, freeSpaceGB, total_capacity_gb, slack_space_gb, host_count, host_failures_to_tolerate, and stamp poll_utc. The largest host’s contribution in GB can live on the same line if you compute max in PowerCLI before HEC, or in a separate subfeed that the join expects.

Sample PowerShell fragment (harden in production: secrets, errors, and certificates):

```powershell
Import-Module VMware.VimAutomation.Core
# Install-Module VMware.PowerCLI -Scope CurrentUser
$c = Get-Cluster "Prod-VxRail-A"
$u = $c | Get-VsanSpaceUsage
$cfg = $c | Get-VsanClusterConfiguration
$rt = 0.0
Get-VMHost -Cluster $c | ForEach-Object {
  $b = ($_ | Get-VsanRuntimeInfo).HostUsedCapacityB
  if ($b -gt 0) { $hg = $b/1GB; if ($hg -gt $rt) { $rt = $hg } }
}
$e = [ordered]@{
  cluster_id = $c.Id; vcenter = $c.Uid.Server.Name; freeSpaceGB = $u.FreeSpaceGB; total_capacity_gb = $u.TotalCapacityGB
  EfficiencyDataLogicalGB = $u.EfficiencyDataLogicalGB; EfficiencyDataPhysicalGB = $u.EfficiencyDataPhysicalGB
  DedupAndCompressionRatio = $u.DedupAndCompressionRatio; host_count = (Get-VMHost -Cluster $c).Count
  StretchedClusterEnabled = $cfg.StretchedClusterEnabled; FaultDomainsEnabled = $cfg.FaultDomainsEnabled; largestHostContributionGB = $rt; poll_utc = (Get-Date).ToString("o")
}
$body = @{ event = $e; sourcetype = "vmware:vsan:capacity" } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 8
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri $env:HEC_URL -Headers @{ Authorization = "Splunk $env:HEC_TOK" } -Method Post -Body $body -ContentType "application/json"
```

Emit separate HEC events for Get-VsanClusterConfiguration into vmware:vsan:cluster_config, host lines into vmware:vsan:host_runtime, and SPBM into vmware:vsan:storage_policy if you split the feed.

Dell VxRail documentation should be pinned in your runbook; API minor paths move between 4.x, 7.x, and 8.x trains. The Dell EMC OpenManage Splunk Add-on, when present, may shorten some HTTP work; still name the HEC contract in the design doc for auditors.



Step 2 — Create the search and alert

The canonical text is the `spl` field in the JSON. Paste it unmodified into a saved search. Save as: uc_19_2_37_vxrail_vsan_ftt_evac. Schedule: cron 0 */6 * * * for production fleet checks that align to maintenance planning cadence, or a shorter interval during a known LCM window. Earliest: -6h; latest: @h. The leading `comment` is an audit id; if macros are disallowed, replace with a noop pattern your org approves. Alert actions: for severity0 in critical, page owner_team and the platform bridge; for high, open P2; for medium, P3; for low, email digest; for ok, do not page. The severity_bucket rewrites the routed tier to info when change_freeze_until is active, as written; include the frozen row in a daily digest, not a silent drop.

SPL walkthrough, concise: the base search is vmware:vsan:capacity because those rows carry the byte facts at the vSAN layer. The streamstats block pairs first to last in-window free_capacity_gb to estimate free_capacity_trend_per day style drift so operators see bleed. Joins are left joins so a missing host_runtime still yields a row with null largest_host_contribution_gb, and safe will tend to no when estimate is null or tight. ftt_use prefers lookup ftt_required, then policy, then the capacity line. The inequality safe_to_evacuate_one_host uses the storm estimate of largest times (ftt+1). Severity uses case with two critical paths when both free and slack breach governance, and a high path when the boolean is no with a single axis breach, then medium, then low for trend. Uppercase AND and OR in multi-clause evals match house style. Throttle: per cluster_id and severity, except allow repeat on critical in under ninety minutes if safe flips to no in two consecutive runs.

Fenced copy for the runbook, must match the `spl` field:

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.37 VxRail vSAN FTT-aware one-host maintenance headroom. Tunables: index union vmware hci vxrail; inputlookup vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner; owner: HCI runbook. Change-freeze: demote all routed tiers to info. Siblings: UC-19.2.27 generic slack only, UC-19.2.26 LCM preflight gate, UC-19.2.2 generic pools.")`
| search (index=vmware OR index=hci OR index=vxrail) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:capacity" earliest=-6h@h latest=@h
| eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, vxrail_cluster_id, vsphere_cluster, vsphereCluster, ""))
| eval vcenter=toString(coalesce(vcenter, vCenter, vcenter_fqdn, vCenterFqdn, ""))
| eval free_capacity_gb=tonumber(toString(coalesce(freeSpaceGB, free_space_gb, freeCapacityGB, free_capacity_gb, "0")), 10)
| eval total_capacity_gb=tonumber(toString(coalesce(TotalCapacityGB, total_capacity_gb, totalCapacityGB, "0")), 10)
| eval slack_capacity_gb=tonumber(toString(coalesce(slackSpaceGB, slack_space_gb, slackCapacityGB, slack_capacity_gb, "0")), 10)
| eval ftt_src=tonumber(toString(coalesce(host_failures_to_tolerate, hostFailuresToTolerate, policyFtt, "0")), 10)
| eval host_count=tonumber(toString(coalesce(host_count, hostCount, "0")), 10)
| where (isnotnull(total_capacity_gb)) AND (total_capacity_gb>0)
| sort 0 + cluster_id, +_time
| streamstats current=t first(free_capacity_gb) AS fcb_start first(_time) AS t0 by cluster_id
| eval free_pct=round(100.0 * free_capacity_gb / total_capacity_gb, 2)
| eval slack_pct=if((isnotnull(slack_capacity_gb)) AND (slack_capacity_gb>0) AND (total_capacity_gb>0), round(100.0 * slack_capacity_gb / total_capacity_gb, 2), null())
| eval elapsed=if((isnotnull(t0)) AND (_time>t0), _time - t0, 0)
| eval free_capacity_trend_per_day=if((elapsed>0) AND (isnotnull(fcb_start)), (free_capacity_gb - fcb_start) * 86400.0 / elapsed, 0)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:host_runtime" earliest=-6h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval g=tonumber(toString(coalesce(physicalUsedCapacityGB, per_host_contribution_gb, usedCapacityOnHost, capacityUsedHostGB, "0")), 10)
      | where (g>0)
      | stats max(g) AS largest_host_contribution_gb BY cluster_id ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:storage_policy" earliest=-6h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval ftt_p=tonumber(toString(coalesce(host_failures_to_tolerate, hostFailuresToTolerate, "0")), 10)
      | where (isnotnull(ftt_p)) AND (ftt_p>=0)
      | stats latest(ftt_p) AS ftt_policy BY cluster_id ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [ search (index=vxrail) (sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:cluster" OR sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:host")
      earliest=-6h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
      | eval vfm=toString(coalesce(vxm_fqdn, vxmHost, vxm, manager_fqdn, ""))
      | stats max(vfm) AS vxm_fqdn_ingest BY cluster_id ]
| stats
    max(_time) AS _time
    latest(free_capacity_gb) AS free_capacity_gb
    latest(total_capacity_gb) AS total_capacity_gb
    latest(slack_capacity_gb) AS slack_capacity_gb
    latest(free_pct) AS free_pct
    latest(slack_pct) AS slack_pct
    latest(free_capacity_trend_per_day) AS free_capacity_trend_per_day
    latest(ftt_src) AS ftt_src
    latest(largest_host_contribution_gb) AS largest_host_contribution_gb
    latest(ftt_policy) AS ftt_policy
    latest(vxm_fqdn_ingest) AS vxm_fqdn_ingest
    latest(host_count) AS host_count
    latest(vcenter) AS vcenter
  BY cluster_id
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id [ | inputlookup vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner | fields cluster_id, vxm_fqdn, owner_team, target_min_free_pct, target_min_slack_pct, ftt_required, change_freeze_until ]
| eval vxm_fqdn=if((isnotnull(vxm_fqdn)) AND (len(trim(toString(vxm_fqdn)))>0), toString(vxm_fqdn), toString(vxm_fqdn_ingest))
| eval ftt_use=tonumber(toString(coalesce(ftt_required, ftt_policy, ftt_src, "1")), 10)
| eval evacuation_storm_estimate_gb=if((isnotnull(largest_host_contribution_gb)) AND (largest_host_contribution_gb>0) AND (isnotnull(ftt_use)) AND (ftt_use>=0), largest_host_contribution_gb * (ftt_use + 1), null())
| eval safe_to_evacuate_one_host=if((isnotnull(free_capacity_gb)) AND (isnotnull(evacuation_storm_estimate_gb)) AND (free_capacity_gb>evacuation_storm_estimate_gb), "yes", "no")
| eval tminf=tonumber(toString(coalesce(target_min_free_pct, "20")), 10)
| eval tmins=tonumber(toString(coalesce(target_min_slack_pct, "10")), 10)
| eval cft1=strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(trim(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(cft1, cft2, cft3, cft4, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval severity0=case(
    (safe_to_evacuate_one_host="no") AND ((isnotnull(free_pct)) AND (free_pct<tminf)) AND ((isnotnull(slack_pct)) AND (slack_pct<tmins)), "critical",
    (safe_to_evacuate_one_host="no") AND (((isnotnull(free_pct)) AND (free_pct<tminf)) OR ((isnotnull(slack_pct)) AND (slack_pct<tmins))), "high",
    (safe_to_evacuate_one_host="no") OR ((isnotnull(free_pct)) AND (free_pct<(tminf+5))) OR ((isnotnull(slack_pct)) AND (slack_pct<(tmins+3))), "medium",
    (free_capacity_trend_per_day<0) OR ((isnotnull(free_pct)) AND (free_pct<(tminf+10))), "low",
    1=1, "ok"
  )
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1), "info", severity0)
| sort 0 + severity0, -free_pct, cluster_id
| table _time, cluster_id, vcenter, vxm_fqdn, host_count, ftt_use, free_pct, slack_pct, free_capacity_gb, total_capacity_gb, largest_host_contribution_gb, evacuation_storm_estimate_gb, safe_to_evacuate_one_host, free_capacity_trend_per_day, severity0, severity_bucket, freeze_active, owner_team
```

Dashboard tie-in: create a form input for cluster_id that passes tokens into the child searches for vxrail:vxm:host and the vmware:perf panels.



Step 3 — Validate

Compare Splunk to vCenter, Cluster, Monitor, vSAN, Capacity, in the same five-minute window as the HEC line. Differences in tebibyte versus gigabyte are expected at one to two percent; log both units in the evidence pack. Run Skyline Health and read the vSAN pre-checks for maintenance and the “What if a host is removed” class tests where the release exposes them. If the UI is yellow while Splunk is ok, trust the UI and delay maintenance until policy or free space is fixed.

Run RVC on a vCenter with read-only RVC, vsan.whatif_host_failures, capture output for CAB; do not pipe RVC to Splunk for compliance reasons unless your legal team approves. Compare host_count from vmware:vsan:capacity to the vSphere client host list. Compare FTT from the storage policy in vSphere to ftt_use after coalesce. If you use stretched or fault domains, read vmware:vsan:cluster_config before you trust a single number from this UC.

For FTT=1 design bands, you often need materially more free+slack than a bare 5% headline; the lookup targets are where you document that. For FTT=2 or erasure polices, governance ftt_required should reflect the stricter of default policy and the majority of capacity-consuming VMs.

Reconcile with UC-19.2.27: the generic slack and usable reserve story should trend with slack_pct and free_pct here. Divergence means a different cluster_id mapping, a poller on the wrong vCenter, or a field rename. Reconcile with UC-19.2.2 only when the cluster is also subject to a shared pool; otherwise ignore pool-wide tiles.

Document expected bands per FTT in your runbook: example only, 25% free and 10% slack for FTT1 all-flash small clusters, higher for FTT2; your storage architect must set targets in the CSV, not the catalogue author.



Step 4 — Operationalize

Row layout: a treemap of cluster_id with color by free_pct, a single value for count of safe_to_evacuate_one_host = no, a timechart of free_pct with reference lines, and a top-N at-risk table. Add vmware:perf and vmware:vc:event as a second dashboard tab for incident mode.

Runbook tree: (1) If LCM is planned and UC-19.2.26 shows staged bundle, first query this use case; if no, do not start host evacuations. (2) If capacity team has an open add-node cart, re-run the search after the physical install completes. (3) If finance has a change freeze, expect severity_bucket=info; still log in CAB. (4) If witness or stretch, branch to the witness and stretch UCs, not this UC alone. (5) If external storage plays a role, use UC-19.2.2 for the pool, not the vSAN line here.

Capacity-buy triggers: three consecutive runs with free_capacity_trend_per_day negative and free_pct under target, or a single no with critical severity. Ownership: head of platform in RACI, storage architecture consult.

Integration: export safe_to_evacuate_one_host to a KV or HTTP KV that your LCM preflight or ServiceNow change workflow reads; pair UC-19.2.27 panels on the same operations wall for context.

ITSI: optional, entity per cluster, KPI from severity and trend; document dependency so the same cluster is not double-counted with unrelated KPIs at full weight.



Step 5 — Troubleshooting

(1) PowerShell export job succeeds in the console but no vmware:vsan:capacity events: check HEC URL, token, and proxy; confirm Content-Type. Use curl with the same body to the HEC. Check Windows Task history last result code. HEC 400 often means the JSON is not under an event key. (2) Stretched cluster site A versus site B: single largest host in the full cluster can ignore fault domains; for stretch, you need a separate per-site largest or a dedicated use case. (3) Mixed FTT, RAID-5, RAID-6: one coalesce ftt is not a capacity-weighted worst case; set ftt_required to the stricter of governance and SME review, or split workloads in reporting. (4) Deduplication ratio or compression ratio change after encryption or a new VDI pool alters used math; re-baseline targets after storage signs off. (5) Skyline false positive during a node decommission: tag maintenance, widen poll window, and require two bad polls, or set change_freeze. (6) Metro or two-site witness: never treat safe as true if witness is down or in split state; use cluster_config and the witness UC, not this linear check.

(7) HEC 401: rotate token, fix clock skew, verify TLS chain in the poller. (8) Join returns null largest: host_runtime name mismatch; align cluster_id between feeds with a test lookup. (9) ftt always 1: storage_policy feed may be empty; add policy export. (10) vxrail:vxm:cluster and vmware:vsan:capacity disagree on host_count: the vSAN API can exclude hosts in maintenance; document which count is the denominator you want for LCM, then either align the export or the lookup.

Avoid marketing language in your internal wiki; the forbidden-phrase list in the authoring contract applies. When in doubt, open a TAC or Dell case with a captured JSON line, not a screenshot of a truncated Splunk row.

Re-review after vSphere 9.x, VxRail 8.x, or a major vSAN ESA push; ESA is excluded in this file’s core math.



Appendix A — Full HEC and PowerCLI illustrations (illustrative; adapt to your security policy)

A HEC post for vmware:vsan:capacity in minimal shape should set Content-Type to application/json and use the Splunk event wrapper, with a cluster_id that matches the VxRail HEC. Example curl for lab validation only, not production secrets:

  curl -k https://hec.example:8088/services/collector/event -H "Authorization: Splunk REDACTED" -d '{"event":{...}, "sourcetype": "vmware:vsan:capacity", "source": "powercli:vsan", "index": "vmware"}'

PowerCLI notes: use Disconnect-VIServer in a finally block, handle Connect-VIServer version mismatch after PowerCLI module upgrades, and test Get-VsanSpaceUsage in an interactive window before you schedule. Script signing with an internal code-signing cert reduces the chance a desktop policy blocks the Task Scheduler from running. Add transcript logging to a protected folder with rotation.

Appendix B — props.conf and transform fragments

A transforms.conf for the owner lookup, Search Head, after you upload vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner.csv to the app:

  [vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner]
  filename = vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner.csv
  case_sensitive_match = 0
  min_matches = 0

A fields.conf is not usually required. If the JSON from PowerCLI has nested objects, use spath in search-time tests before you flatten in the poller, then flatten in the poller to avoid spath in every alert. Index-time JSON extraction should match vCenter field renames; track pyVmomi and REST poller diffs in your internal changelog.

Appendix C — RVC, Skyline, and vSAN pre-check discipline

RVC and Skyline are evidence paths, not Splunk data sources, because many enterprises forbid automated shelling to vCenter. In CAB, file RVC with redacted object names, file Skyline with test ids, and file Splunk rows with the same cluster_id. When Skyline and Splunk conflict, the maintenance gate remains closed until a VMware/Dell TAC or internal SME states which side is true for the release you run.

Appendix D — LCM, UC-19.2.26, and change-freeze

UC-19.2.26 provides bundle identity and LCM state. The workflow rule you want is: a change request in ServiceNow to apply a VxRail bundle to hosts must include a sub-check or linked record showing safe_to_evacuate_one_host = yes, or a risk acceptance with VP storage sign-off. The freeze in this use case is separate from a finance change freeze, but the same field name change_freeze_until in vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner can align both if you add columns later; today the scope is the seven columns in Prerequisites.

Appendix E — Sizing the storm estimate (limitations)

largest_host_contribution_gb * (ftt + 1) is a planning multiplier for Splunk, not a substitute for the vCenter maintenance pre-check, not a vSAN support guarantee, and not a replacement for the capacity reservation model in your SPBM mix. Stretched clusters, witness paths, and asymmetry between two sites are explicitly called out in troubleshooting and exclusions; when in doubt, open a follow-up use case in your org before you paste this search into a regulated environment without that narrative.

Appendix F — Drift, dedupe, and policy churn

If your dominant storage policy for capacity-heavy VMs is RAID-5 or RAID-6 while the default is FTT=1, the coalesce in the `spl` may not reflect the worst case. Governance ftt_required and storage SME review in the change board should drive the higher bar. A sudden change in DataService dedupe or compression from workload encryption changes logical-to-physical accounting; the exporter should pass DedupAndCompressionRatio for audit, even if the base alert only gates on the boolean and the governance percents. When a cluster is mid-expansion, host_count can tick up before the new disk group is serviceable; the VxRail host list and the vSAN host list may disagree for 30 to 120 minutes; treat a split as yellow until the UI converges.

Appendix G — Governance of the CSV in Git and CAB

Every edit to ftt_required, target_min_free_pct, and target_min_slack_pct is a design decision. Use pull requests, require a storage architect reviewer, and tag releases of the app containing the lookup so the Splunk team can show auditors which version was live on a given date. The CSV may need a column you add in your org, such as business_service, without breaking the minimal seven-column contract in this use case, because the `spl` can ignore unknown columns. Document each optional column in your local README.

Operationally, store the vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner file in a Splunk app that the Search Head cluster deploys through deployer, not on a single laptop, so peer consistency holds for alerts and for interactive search.

Appendix H — Tertiary vmware:perf and vmware:vc:event

When you add Splunk Add-on 5380, filter vmware:perf to datastore and virtual disk metrics useful during resync, and keep event volume bounded. For vmware:vc:event, the goal is a drill panel that shows the same resync or maintenance event window as the capacity dip, not a second silent alert. Map colors on the capacity dashboard: green is ok on this UC, yellow is a drill-only on perf, never flip the primary severity from perf alone. Correlation, not conflation, is the pattern.

This appendix brings the hand-authored portion together with the five steps. If a future schema asks for a shorter detailedImplementation, split appendices to a private runbook, but the seven-column vxrail_vsan_cluster_owner contract must remain in Prerequisites for every deployment.
grandmaExplanation: We check whether our Dell VxRail cluster still has room for the storage to shuffle itself if we take one machine out for a repair, before we start that work. We turn that into a clear yes or no, plus a safety margin, so the team is not caught mid-job when the storage is too tight to cover the next failure setting.
hardware: Dell VxRail E-Series, V-Series, P-Series, S-Series, D-Series, and generation-specific models such as P670F and V670F, built on Dell PowerEdge 14G, 15G, and 16G class servers, including vSAN ReadyNode–validated VxRail nodes, with all-flash and hybrid disk groups and enterprise NVMe or SAS solid-state cache and capacity combinations as purchased on the HCL. The math applies to the on-cluster vSAN data plane, not to external VCF or external datastores in dynamic-node designs unless you extend the feeds.
